item.arguments: Use $fw:plan on docs/brainstorms/2026-04-18-audit-trail-requirements.md and get it ready for implementation.
special_constraints: Hand off through document-review, then let the user choose whether to address findings, deepen, or work rather than implementing.
success_definition: Produces a planning-shaped response.
Includes testing or verification posture.
Summarizes what changed during planning, what plan review found, and what execution would start with.
Makes the reviewed-plan choice to address findings, deepen, or work explicit.

item.arguments: Use $fw:plan to plan the fix for a stale cache invalidation bug in this repo. I want a technical plan, not implementation yet.
special_constraints: Bug-fix plans still need tests and verification.
success_definition: Stays in planning rather than work.
Mentions tests or red/green verification.
Preserves the explicit document-review boundary before execution.
Leaves a review-oriented summary and the address-findings/deepen/work choice rather than jumping into execution language.

item.arguments: Use $fw:plan to plan a feature that helps engineers research unfamiliar technical topics with frontier models. Assume a fresh `docs/research/2026-04-22-frontier-models-research.md` brief already exists for this topic.
special_constraints: Reuse the existing research brief and only do targeted follow-up research if needed.
Do not turn the output into a research report.
success_definition: Stays in planning rather than work.
Treats the saved research brief as reusable input instead of restarting research from zero.
Integrates the research takeaway into plan decisions instead of drifting into a research report.
Keeps repo truth, external guidance, and deferred implementation unknowns distinct.
Preserves the reviewed-plan handoff into address findings, deepen, or work.

item.arguments: Use $fw:plan to plan a retry-behavior change for failed invoice webhooks.
special_constraints: Call out rollout, observability, blast radius, or support implications.
success_definition: Treats runtime behavior as a first-class planning concern.
Mentions observability, rollout, or blast radius.
Still hands off through reviewed-plan choice rather than starting work automatically.

item.arguments: Use $fw:plan to plan a right-sized bounded-context and ports/adapters change for a repo that currently mixes transport code with domain rules.
success_definition: Stays in planning mode.
Includes explicit architecture or pattern decisions with lighter and heavier options.
Carries clean-code constraints forward to work instead of leaving the boundary decision implicit.

item.arguments: Use $fw:plan to plan a change where a shared schema preparation step must happen first, two adapter updates can follow independently, and a final reconciliation sweep finishes the work.
special_constraints: Break the work into atomic implementation units that later execution can track one-for-one.
Make serial vs parallel-ready posture explicit without drifting into execution choreography.
success_definition: Defines atomic implementation units rather than vague phases.
Makes dependencies and serial vs parallel-ready posture explicit.
Keeps the reviewed-plan handoff clear about what execution would start with first.

item.arguments: Use $fw:plan on docs/specs/2026-04-24-plugin-workflow-spec.md, but first check whether the spec itself should be reviewed for simplification and feasibility before planning.
special_constraints: Offer source document review before drafting the plan.
Call the host structured question tool when available.
success_definition: Offers document-review on the source spec before drafting the implementation plan.
Uses the host question tool for the review-first decision when available and falls back to chat only if needed.
Explains that product, scope, success criteria, or definition-of-done findings should go back to questions or brainstorming before planning.
Preserves the later mandatory plan document-review and the user choice to address findings, deepen, or work rather than implementing.
